_comment: Auto-dismiss rules for XAE modal dialogs that would otherwise block te1000 MCP calls. The daemon's in-process dialog watcher (daemon/DialogWatcher.cs) tries each rule in order against any modal dialog it detects; the FIRST match wins and its button is clicked. A dialog that matches no rule is SURFACED to the calling agent (the MCP call fails with the dialog's title/text/buttons) so the agent can ask the user how to resolve it, then call 'xae dialog_resolve {button, remember?}' to click the chosen button on the live dialog. dialog_resolve with remember:true APPENDS a rule here AND hot-applies it to the running watcher (no daemon restart needed). Disruptive prompts are refused for auto-remember (see _safety). Ships EMPTY (report-only) by default — a fresh clone auto-clicks NOTHING; build the list up yourself, by hand or via dialog_resolve {button, remember:true}.

_rule_format.match: REQUIRED regex tested (case-insensitive, -match) against the dialog TITLE
_rule_format.textMatch: OPTIONAL regex tested against the dialog BODY text; rule only fires if this also matches
_rule_format.button: EXACT button label to click (the '&' accelerator is ignored, case-insensitive). e.g. 'Yes', 'No', 'OK'

_safety: These rules cause buttons to be clicked in your XAE instance automatically and unattended — only add dialogs you are certain are safe to auto-answer. NEVER add rules for Activate Configuration / Run-mode / restart / download / boot project / TwinSAFE / safety prompts — those must stay human-confirmed. This is enforced in two places: (1) 'xae dialog_resolve' REFUSES to persist a remember rule when remember:true matches a disruptive prompt (activate.*config / run-mode / restart / download / boot project / twinsafe / safety / set.*run / reset.*twincat) — the one-time human-chosen click still happens and it reports rememberRefused with the reason; and (2) the watcher itself never auto-clicks a dialog matching that pattern, even if a rule here would otherwise match it. Prefer matching the non-destructive button (No/Cancel) when in doubt.
